mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or positive returns occur in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in >65% of recovery-phase observations, or the predictive power vanishes when filtered to non-recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or increasing federal funds rate indicates real interest rate tightening that compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of instances where CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS does not fall, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained economic growth and rising consumer demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months following payroll gains, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP exceeding 0.5% signals an economic contraction that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: The market produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered quarters, or the signal fails to achieve a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects resilient manufacturing output that sustains equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of late-cycle observations with rising INDPRO, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.
